task: Diagnose suspected OPS-proxy regression in scripts/baseline_comparison.py

investigation.claim_under_review: scripts/baseline_comparison.py:166-170 still uses OPS-based WAR proxy as comparison target instead of real bWAR.
investigation.finding: Real bWAR is already live as the comparison target. The OPS proxy code at scripts/baseline_comparison.py:165-170 (batters) and :230-237 (pitchers) is dormant fallback gated by `has_real_war = df['war'].notna().any()` (line 136 / line 213). In the current DB this condition is True for every test season, so the fallback never fires.

evidence.note: has_real_war = df['war'].notna().any() -- True when any row has a populated bWAR.
When has_real_war is True, trad_war_season := df['war'] and war_source := 'season_batting_stats.war'.
OPS-proxy branch -- only reached when ALL war values in the season window are NULL. Currently unreachable on 2023-2024.
Same gating for pitchers; war_source := 'season_pitching_stats.war' when bWAR populated.

16,583 rows; cols = [player_id, player_name, season, position_type, war, pa_or_ip, war_source]. The bWAR is in column `war` (NOT `bref_bwar` as the prior researcher reported); `war_source` is a string label set to 'bref_bwar'. Backfill from this parquet into the DB columns is already complete (commit 11d74c5).
data_quality.batter_war_source = 'season_batting_stats.war', pitcher_war_source = 'season_pitching_stats.war'. Combined ρ=0.62 was already measured against real bWAR, not the OPS proxy.
Backfill commit landed cleanly: changed 8 files, populated DB columns, retained OPS code only as documented fallback.

investigation.researcher_misread_explanation: The prior DML-confounder agent appears to have read the OPS-proxy branch literally without inspecting the gating condition above it, and assumed the bWAR column name in the staging parquet was `bref_bwar` (it is actually `war`, with `war_source = 'bref_bwar'` as a label).

code_changes: NONE -- bWAR was already live; no regression to fix.

spec_gate_check.gate: combined Spearman rho 95% CI lower bound >= 0.60

cohort_sizes_pa100_ip20.after_actual_fix: N/A -- no fix made; bWAR already live

notes: Real bWAR is the comparison target now and was when the 161738Z baseline ran. The fragile combined ρ lower CI (0.577) is NOT caused by an OPS-proxy regression; it is a real model-side limitation. Switching the target away from OPS already happened in commit 11d74c5; expected gain from the swap was already booked into the 161738Z numbers. To clear the mint gate (lower CI >= 0.60) the model itself (or the cohort definition / aggregation) needs improvement -- that is the next agent's task.
